current.seoTitle: About GetForked | Own Your Business Workflows
current.metaDescription: GetForked was started by business owners and developers who replaced their own SaaS stack, then built a practical way to help others own the workflow.
current.h1: We built GetForked after replacing our own SaaS stack
current.heroSummary: GetForked began with business owners and developers who were tired of paying the SaaS tax for disconnected tools, fragile automation, and workflows held together by spreadsheets.
points: We experienced the SaaS tax ourselves
We built our own replacements first
Now we help other owners own the workflow

sections.heading: From rented software to owned workflows
paragraphs: We replaced bloated CRMs, spreadsheet dashboards, fragile middleware, and manual handoffs with direct connectors, internal tools, and AI-assisted workflows that kept humans in control where judgment mattered.

sections.heading: Why GetForked exists
paragraphs: GetForked helps business owners reduce software waste, scope practical workflow replacements, and match the work with approved builders who understand handover-ready business systems.

faq.question: Who started GetForked?
faq.answer: GetForked was started by business owners and developers who first built their own replacements for rented SaaS tools, dashboards, automations, and internal workflows.

faq.question: What does own the workflow mean?
faq.answer: It means the business has clearer control over the logic, data, handover, and daily process instead of depending on another subscription for every operational gap.

writerBrief.likelySearcher: A business owner or operator evaluating owned workflow replacement.
pageMustAnswer: What is this page about? We built GetForked after replacing our own SaaS stack
How does it connect to owning workflows?
What should the visitor do next?
writerBrief.informationGain: This page should make /about/ useful as a standalone GetForked page, not just a generic company page.
requiredEntities: We built GetForked after replacing our own SaaS stack
We experienced the SaaS tax ourselves
We built our own replacements first
Now we help other owners own the workflow
avoidRepeating: Stop renting software. Start owning the workflow.
GetForked matches your business with approved builders who replace wasteful SaaS subscriptions, messy spreadsheets, and manual handoffs with custom tools built around how your business actually runs.
From workflow problem to owned system in six guided steps
Submit a structured brief once, then GetForked reviews the workflow, clarifies scope, matches the project with an approved builder, and keeps delivery tied to practical milestones.
Replace software waste with workflows your business can own
Explain the workflow pain once, then get matched with approved builders who can turn subscriptions, spreadsheets, and manual handoffs into a practical owned system.
Build owned workflows for real business projects
Join the approved builder network to receive scoped projects, practical expectations, and operational support without chasing vague leads or competing in bid spam.
Every builder is reviewed before project invitations
Approved builders are reviewed for communication, AI workflow capability, practical tooling fit, delivery standards, and handover expectations before they enter matching.
toneRules: Write like practical business owners and developers, not a generic software agency.
Use own the workflow and reduce the SaaS tax where natural.
Do not invent savings, client counts, timelines, guarantees, or proprietary process claims.
Keep GetForked positioned as scoped brief plus approved builder matching.
